legal_model.notes_normalized: Cannabis is decriminalized in Mexico / México. Medical cannabis is legal. Distribution is illegal.

notes_normalized: Cannabis is decriminalized in Mexico / México. Medical cannabis is legal. Distribution is illegal.
notes_raw: {{Main|Cannabis in Mexico}} Possession of {{cvt|5|g|frac=16}} was decriminalized in 2009.<ref>{{cite news |url = https://www.nytimes.com/2009/08/21/world/americas/21mexico.html |work = [[The New York Times]] |agency = [[Associated Press]] |title = Mexico Legalizes Drug Possession |date = 21 August 2009 |access-date = 4 April 2010 |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20180401103237/https://www.nytimes.com/2009/08/21/world/americas/21mexico.html |archive-date = 1 April 2018 |url-status = live}}</ref> In 2015 the Supreme Court voted 4–1 that prohibiting people from growing the drug for personal use was unconstitutional.<ref>{{cite news |url = https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-us-canada-34726693 |publisher = [[BBC News]] |title = Mexico court ruling could eventually lead to legal marijuana |date = 4 November 2015 |access-date = 5 November 2015 |archive-date = 3 December 2017 |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20171203223457/http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-us-canada-34726693 |url-status = live }}</ref> In 2018 the Supreme Court reaffirmed the ruling and requested that the legislature update the country's laws regarding cannabis.<ref>{{cite news |last=McDonnell-Parry |first=Amelia |title=Did Mexico Just Legalize Pot? |url=https://www.rollingstone.com/culture/culture-news/mexico-marijuana-legal-decriminalize-pot-weed-751030/ |access-date=14 January 2019 |magazine=[[Rolling Stone]] |date=2 November 2018 |archive-date=15 January 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190115023120/https://www.rollingstone.com/culture/culture-news/mexico-marijuana-legal-decriminalize-pot-weed-751030/ |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{cite news |last=Adlin |first=Ben |title=FAQ: Mexico Legalized Cannabis? Not Exactly |url=https://www.leafly.com/news/politics/is-marijuana-legal-in-mexico-after-supreme-court-ruling |access-date=14 January 2019 |work=[[Leafly]] |date=1 November 2018 |archive-date=15 January 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190115023146/https://www.leafly.com/news/politics/is-marijuana-legal-in-mexico-after-supreme-court-ruling |url-status=live }}</ref> After the legislature failed to act, the Supreme Court in 2021 invalidated laws regarding the personal possession and cultivation of cannabis.<ref name="mexico062821" />

facts.possession_limit: personal possession and cultivation of cannabis.
facts.cultivation: growing the drug for personal use was unconstitutional.
